title: aquilegia coerulea subsp. pinetorum
intro: Aquilegia coerulea subsp. pinetorum, commonly known as the rock columbine, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buttercup family. It is a perennial herb with blue and white flowers and is native to mountainous regions in the western United States. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce swelling and prevent infection. Herbal preparations of rock columbine, such as tinctures and infusions, can be used to treat a variety of health issues, including respiratory problems and skin conditions, due to its expectorant and antimicrobial properties.
